description: Synthetic knowledge items for demonstrating per-turn retrieval.

items.title: Service overview
items.content: The service starts with a short needs conversation, followed by a review of the available next steps.

items.title: Process next steps
items.content: After the initial conversation, the assistant explains the process and asks only for the information needed for the next review step.

items.title: Pricing boundary
items.content: The assistant may explain how pricing, cost, and fee details are assessed, but a binding quote requires a qualified human review.

items.title: Privacy and minimum data
items.content: The assistant should explain why a data point is needed and allow the customer to decline information that is not required for the current step.

items.title: Human handoff
items.content: A customer can request a human representative at any time. The assistant should acknowledge the request and hand off with the relevant conversation context.

items.title: Stop-contact request
items.content: When a customer asks to stop contact, the assistant should acknowledge the request, stop the conversation, and avoid making another offer.

items.title: Callback window
items.content: The assistant may record a broad callback window such as tomorrow or next week. It should avoid collecting unnecessary sensitive details in the scheduling step.

items.title: Identity statement
items.content: The assistant should clearly explain the service it represents and should not claim to be a human or an official authority when it is not.

items.title: Complaint handling
items.content: A complaint should receive a short acknowledgement and an appropriate human escalation path when the customer wants follow-up.

items.title: Unsupported question handling
items.content: When no relevant knowledge item is found, the assistant should ask one clarification question and place the gap into the improvement queue for review.
